segments.text: Now as Jesus passed by, He saw a man who was blind from birth.
And His disciples asked Him, saying,
Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: Jesus answered,
Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but that the works of God should be revealed in him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I must work the works of Him who sent Me while it is day; the night is coming when no one can work.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When He had said these things, He spat on the ground and made clay with the saliva; and He anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay.
And He said to him,
Go, wash in the pool of Siloam
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: (which is translated, Sent). So he went and washed, and came back seeing.
Therefore the neighbors and those who previously had seen that he was blind said,
Is not this he who sat and begged?
segments.speaker: Neighbors

segments.text: This is he.
segments.speaker: Some people

segments.text: He is like him.
segments.speaker: Others

segments.text: I am he.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: Therefore they said to him,
How were your eyes opened?
segments.speaker: Neighbors

segments.text: He answered and said,
A Man called Jesus made clay and anointed my eyes and said to me, 'Go to the pool of Siloam and wash.' So I went and washed, and I received sight.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: Then they said to him,
Where is He?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: I do not know.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

verses.title: The Pharisees Excommunicate the Healed Man

segments.text: They brought him who formerly was blind to the Pharisees.
Now it was a Sabbath when Jesus made the clay and opened his eyes.
Then the Pharisees also asked him again how he had received his sight.
He put clay on my eyes, and I washed, and I see.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: This Man is not from God, because He does not keep the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: Some Pharisees

segments.text: How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?
segments.speaker: Other Pharisees

segments.text: And there was a division among them.
They said to the blind man again,
What do you say about Him because He opened your eyes?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: He is a prophet.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind and received his sight, until they called the parents of him who had received his sight.
And they asked them, saying,
Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
segments.speaker: Parents

segments.text: But by what means he now sees we do not know, or who opened his eyes we do not know. He is of age; ask him. He will speak for himself.
segments.speaker: Parents

segments.text: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews, for the Jews had agreed already that if anyone confessed that He was Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.
He is of age; ask him.
segments.speaker: Parents

segments.text: So they again called the man who was blind, and said to him,
Give God the glory! We know that this Man is a sinner.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: Whether He is a sinner or not I do not know. One thing I know: that though I was blind, now I see.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: Then they said to him again,
What did He do to you? How did He open your eyes?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: I told you already, and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become His disciples?
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: Then they reviled him and said,
You are His disciple, but we are Moses' disciples.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: We know that God spoke to Moses; as for this fellow, we do not know where He is from.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: Why, this is a marvelous thing, that you do not know where He is from; yet He has opened my eyes!
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: Now we know that God does not hear sinners; but if anyone is a worshiper of God and does His will, He hears him.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: Since the world began it has been unheard of that anyone opened the eyes of one who was born blind.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: If this Man were not from God, He could do nothing.
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: They answered and said to him,
You were completely born in sins, and are you teaching us?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: And they cast him out.
Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when He had found him, He said to him,
Do you believe in the Son of God?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He answered and said,
Who is He, Lord, that I may believe in Him?
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: And Jesus said to him,
You have both seen Him and it is He who is talking with you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, I believe!
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: And he worshiped Him.
For judgment I have come into this world, that those who do not see may see, and that those who see may be made blind.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then some of the Pharisees who were with Him heard these words, and said to Him,
Are we blind also?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, 'We see.' Therefore your sin remains.
segments.speaker: Jesus
